page: Vultr vcg-a40-6c-30g-12vram is a Cloud GPU server instance configured with an Intel x86_64 architecture. It features 6 shared vCPUs, 30.0 GB of system memory allocating 5.0 GB per core, and 550 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This server type incorporates a fractional GPU allocation consisting of 0.25 of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, providing 12 GB of dedicated VRAM. The shared CPU allocation and fractional GPU design present a tradeoff, offering lower resource density compared to full-GPU instances but enabling access to hardware acceleration without the cost of a dedicated physical GPU.

The hardware profile is structured for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration alongside balanced system memory and storage. Suitable applications include small-scale machine learning inference, data processing, graphics rendering tasks, and development environments that utilize CUDA or Ampere-specific features without requiring a full dedicated GPU.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-6c-30g-12vram is a Cloud GPU instance built on the Intel x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 6 shared vCPUs, 30.0 GB of system memory, and 550 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance features a fractional GPU configuration, providing 0.25 of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 12 GB of VRAM. This resource allocation offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not justify the expense of a dedicated GPU. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a performance tradeoff for compute-intensive tasks. This server is configured for workloads such as small-scale machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and development environments.

tagline: A fractional GPU instance featuring shared Intel processors, NVMe storage, and a quarter-slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU.
